name: scene_nodes_connect_battle_test
description: Battle-test the imperative-API connect_ports handler against real synthesized capsules. Demonstrates: (a) create_capsule_with_pattern produces working capsules, (b) connect_ports establishes real TYPED wires verifiable via GraphNode.GetConnections(direction=1). NOTE 2026-04-30 (post-GPT-audit): PORTLIST / void-template wires (Memory.nextout -> floatingio.portlist) silently no-op via the Python imperative API — UI auto-port-specialization isn't exposed. PORTLIST connect coverage moved to scene_nodes_floatingio_probe.experimental.json. This green suite covers ONLY typed port-to-port wires (e.g. hash.resultfloat -> compare.in1).

args.script:
import c4d
doc = c4d.documents.GetActiveDocument()
for n in ['BT_Memory', 'BT_Scatter', 'BT_HashSel', 'BT_Loop']:
    o = doc.SearchObject(n)
    if o: o.Remove()
c4d.EventAdd()
print('battle-test setup ok')

import c4d
import maxon
from maxon.frameworks.nodes import GraphDescription
doc = c4d.documents.GetActiveDocument()
cap = doc.SearchObject('BT_Memory')
g = GraphDescription.GetGraph(cap)
# Verify pattern emit produces expected node bases (memory + at least one floatingio).
# Do NOT attempt a Memory.nextout._0 -> floatingio.PORTLIST connect here:
# PORTLIST is a void-template port that silently no-ops on Python imperative
# Connect(). The C4D editor's drag-wire fires a higher-level auto-port-
# specialization that isn't exposed. See scene_nodes_guide.md §8. PORTLIST
# coverage lives in scene_nodes_floatingio_probe.experimental.json.
bases = set()
for c in (g.GetRoot().GetChildren() or []):
    cid = str(c.GetId())
    if '@' in cid and '/' not in cid:
        bases.add(cid.split('@')[0])
if 'memory' not in bases: raise AssertionError(f'BT_Memory missing memory node (bases: {sorted(bases)})')
if 'floatingio' not in bases: raise AssertionError(f'BT_Memory missing floatingio node (bases: {sorted(bases)})')
print(f'MEMORY CAPSULE SYNTHESIS VERIFIED: bases={sorted(bases)}')

import c4d
import maxon
from maxon.frameworks.nodes import GraphDescription
doc = c4d.documents.GetActiveDocument()
cap = doc.SearchObject('BT_Scatter')
g = GraphDescription.GetGraph(cap)
found = False
for c in (g.GetRoot().GetChildren() or []):
    cid = str(c.GetId())
    if '@' in cid and '/' not in cid and cid.split('@')[0] == 'surfacescaledbluenoise':
        found = True; break
if not found: raise AssertionError('BT_Scatter is missing surfacescaledbluenoise')
print('SCATTER VERIFIED: surfacescaledbluenoise present in BT_Scatter')

import c4d
import maxon
from maxon.frameworks.nodes import GraphDescription
doc = c4d.documents.GetActiveDocument()
cap = doc.SearchObject('BT_HashSel')
g = GraphDescription.GetGraph(cap)
hash_node = None
for c in (g.GetRoot().GetChildren() or []):
    cid = str(c.GetId())
    if '@' in cid and '/' not in cid and cid.split('@')[0] == 'hash':
        hash_node = c; break
if not hash_node: raise RuntimeError('hash node not found')
resultfloat = next((p for p in (hash_node.GetOutputs().GetChildren() or []) if str(p.GetId()).endswith('resultfloat')), None)
if not resultfloat: raise RuntimeError('hash.resultfloat port not found')
conns = list(resultfloat.GetConnections(1))
if not conns: raise AssertionError('hash.resultfloat has 0 outgoing connections')
port_targets = [str(p[0].GetId()) for p in conns]
# Lenient match: target port name endswith 'in1' (port-ID format varies — sometimes just 'in1', sometimes prefixed)
found = any(t.endswith('in1') or t == 'in1' for t in port_targets)
if not found: raise AssertionError(f'hash.resultfloat does not target in1 port; got: {port_targets}')
print(f'WIRE VERIFIED: hash.resultfloat -> in1 (targets: {port_targets})')

import c4d
import maxon
from maxon.frameworks.nodes import GraphDescription
doc = c4d.documents.GetActiveDocument()
cap = doc.SearchObject('BT_Loop')
g = GraphDescription.GetGraph(cap)
bases = set()
for c in (g.GetRoot().GetChildren() or []):
    cid = str(c.GetId())
    if '@' in cid and '/' not in cid:
        bases.add(cid.split('@')[0])
required = {'range', 'getcount'}
missing = required - bases
if missing: raise AssertionError(f'BT_Loop missing {missing} (got bases: {sorted(bases)})')
print(f'LOOP VERIFIED: contains {sorted(required)}')

import c4d
doc = c4d.documents.GetActiveDocument()
for n in ['BT_Memory', 'BT_Scatter', 'BT_HashSel', 'BT_Loop']:
    o = doc.SearchObject(n)
    if o: o.Remove()
c4d.EventAdd()
print('teardown ok')
